mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the cost of capital and compress equity discount rates, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 9.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and recessionary pressures, causing investors to sell equities and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE exceeding 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with falling industrial production indicates inventory buildup and weakening demand transmission, which negatively impacts corporate earnings expectations and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, increasing industrial production signals expanding economic output and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Mechanism predicts positive returns but actual 20d forward returns are negative in > 55% of cases during confirmed recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which boosts economic growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or HOUST increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above a 0.3% monthly threshold, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when CPIAUCSL MoM change exceeds 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in total nonfarm payrolls and a decrease in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to outperform a baseline model with a t-statistic below 1.96 over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, rising real GDP growth signals effective stimulus transmission to the real economy, reinforcing bullish equity sentiment and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 60% of cases when GDPC1 rises during confirmed easing periods.
